title: Extreme heat deaths must be avoided
content: Extreme heat deaths in India are predictable and preventable. Early warnings, community action and stronger governance can protect vulnerable workers, reduce inequality‑driven exposure, and ensure heat‑related deaths are properly counted and addressed.

title: Study links past emissions to trillions in future economic damages
content: Future climate damages from past greenhouse gas emissions dwarf the economic harm already inflicted.
snippet: Future climate damages from past greenhouse gas emissions dwarf the economic harm already inflicted.

title: Seychelles country climate and development report
content: The report outlines how Seychelles can achieve sustainable economic growth while strengthening climate resilience in the face of increasing environmental risks.
snippet: The report outlines how Seychelles can achieve sustainable economic growth while strengthening climate resilience in the face of increasing environmental risks.

title: Social influences on behavioural response to earthquake shaking: evidence from CCTV footage
content: This study examines how people respond to earthquake shaking, focusing on the role of social influence in shaping behaviour.
snippet: This study examines how people respond to earthquake shaking, focusing on the role of social influence in shaping behaviour.

title: Urban flood and gully erosion risk management in the Congo basin
content: Early warning systems: Rapid assessments from four partner countries: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Mali, Niger, and South Sudan
snippet: Early warning systems: Rapid assessments from four partner countries: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Mali, Niger, and South Sudan

title: Indonesia’s economic security requires ecological resilience
content: Indonesia’s economic growth is increasingly threatened by climate disasters, highlighting the urgent need to integrate environmental protection and ecological resilience into national economic and policy planning.
snippet: Indonesia’s economic growth is increasingly threatened by climate disasters, highlighting the urgent need to integrate environmental protection and ecological resilience into national economic and pol

title: Cascading impacts of natural disasters in a connected world
content: Extreme climate-related disasters create interconnected cascading impacts across sectors and regions, making it essential to improve global risk modelling and management systems.
snippet: Extreme climate-related disasters create interconnected cascading impacts across sectors and regions, making it essential to improve global risk modelling and management systems.

title: Wildfire risk assessment
content: Explore this collection to learn more about the latest insights on future wildfire trends, the science behind wildfire-related compound events and wildfire-related attribution studies.
snippet: Explore this collection to learn more about the latest insights on future wildfire trends, the science behind wildfire-related compound events and wildfire-related attribution studies.

title: Climate change is projected to cause ten times more people to die in poor countries than rich countries
content: A report on the impact of rising temperatures on mortality underscores the need for targeted investments to help people and communities save lives.
snippet: A report on the impact of rising temperatures on mortality underscores the need for targeted investments to help people and communities save lives.

title: Human health: Measuring the impact of rising temperatures on mortality to target adaptation planning
content: This report identifies the regions around the world where climate adaptation offers the greatest opportunity to save the most lives, based on the Climate Impact Lab’s projections of temperature related mortality in 2050.
snippet: This report identifies the regions around the world where climate adaptation offers the greatest opportunity to save the most lives, based on the Climate Impact Lab’s projections of temperature relate

title: Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth, University of Chicago
content: The Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth leverages the University’s unique legacy and resources to balance the risks of a changing climate with the essential need for human progress.
snippet: The Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth leverages the University’s unique legacy and resources to balance the risks of a changing climate with the essential need for human progress.

title: Institute for Economic Policy Research (SIEPR), Stanford University
content: The Stanford Institute for Economic Policy Research is a nonpartisan economic research institution housed at Stanford University.
snippet: The Stanford Institute for Economic Policy Research is a nonpartisan economic research institution housed at Stanford University.

title: Design of a virtual test bed for the Italian territory: Proof of concept for multi-hazard scenarios effects in the context of a national resilience assessment
content: This study presents a proof‑of‑concept virtual test bed designed to model multi‑hazard scenarios and support national resilience assessment across Italy.
snippet: This study presents a proof‑of‑concept virtual test bed designed to model multi‑hazard scenarios and support national resilience assessment across Italy.

title: From flood risk to resilience: Practical flood adaptation strategies for refugee settlements in Ethiopia and South Sudan
content: This report outlines practical, evidence-based flood risk reduction strategies for two displacement settings in East Africa: Kule Refugee Camp in Ethiopia and Renk Transit Centre in South Sudan.
snippet: This report outlines practical, evidence-based flood risk reduction strategies for two displacement settings in East Africa: Kule Refugee Camp in Ethiopia and Renk Transit Centre in South Sudan.

title: Online Workshop on Strengthening Whole-of-Society Capacities for Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Resilience: Inclusive Governance and Innovative Mechanisms
content: The workshop will introduce concepts, frameworks, practical approaches to strengthen inclusive DRR, climate resilience, and climate action through effective governance, risk-informed planning, private sector engagement, and technology-enabled solutions.
snippet: The workshop will introduce concepts, frameworks, practical approaches to strengthen inclusive DRR, climate resilience, and climate action through effective governance, risk-informed planning, private

title: Intra Africa, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) Disaster Risk Reduction Programme
content: This web-based collection shares local and regional experiences for the benefit of the wider DRR community and OACP countries, produced in the context of the Intra-ACP Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Programme.
snippet: This web-based collection shares local and regional experiences for the benefit of the wider DRR community and OACP countries, produced in the context of the Intra-ACP Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Pr

title: Extreme heat risk perceptions and readiness in schools
content: This study outlines how rising extreme heat poses growing risks to pupils and staff in schools across the United Kingdom, highlighting limited preparedness and the absence of robust heat action plans.
snippet: This study outlines how rising extreme heat poses growing risks to pupils and staff in schools across the United Kingdom, highlighting limited preparedness and the absence of robust heat action plans.

title: Adaptation to rising seas is possible in most deltas… for now
content: A delta’s physical characteristics, such as coastline length or sand availability, can help identify which adaptation measures could be implemented based on current technological capabilities and resource or space availability.

title: Building tomorrow’s grid: managing extreme heat risk in a warming world
content: Extreme heat from climate change is increasingly threatening U.S. energy supply and reliability – forcing energy companies to take steps to safeguard operations and meet rising demand.
snippet: Extreme heat from climate change is increasingly threatening U.S. energy supply and reliability – forcing energy companies to take steps to safeguard operations and meet rising demand.

title: From crisis to resilience: How the village of Kemnay transformed its flood defences
content: In 2016, Storm Frank devastated Kembhill Park in Scotland, flooding 48 homes. MegaSecur witnessed how a passive community transformed itself into a leader of its own governance by implementing Water-Gate technology.

title: Why resilient infrastructure can no longer be optional
content: In his three decades of responding to catastrophic events around the world, Kamal Kishore, head of the United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction, often observed a curious human tendency to overlook the invisible foundations of our safety.

title: Cyclone Narelle is now larger and ‘more severe’ as it crosses the Western Australian coast
content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle continues to amaze with its long journey across northern Australia.
snippet: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle continues to amaze with its long journey across northern Australia.

title: Groundbreaking atmospheric river forecasting collaboration crosses the finish line
content: System delivers more accurate forecasts of rainfall, runoff, and flooding.
snippet: System delivers more accurate forecasts of rainfall, runoff, and flooding.

title: Thousands of kites once filled the skies above India's cities. Today the tradition is dying out
content: Kite flying was once a cherished tradition in many parts of India, with people crowding the rooftops during kite festivals. But recent heatwaves and a number of deaths due to illegal kite strings have seen many abandoning the once popular pastime.
snippet: Kite flying was once a cherished tradition in many parts of India, with people crowding the rooftops during kite festivals. But recent heatwaves and a number of deaths due to illegal kite strings have

title: Gender mainstreaming in WMO-implemented CREWS projects: 2015–2025
content: Gender mainstreaming is an institutional priority for WMO and its partners. The WMO Gender Action Plan (2020–2023) and CREWS Operational Procedures Note No. 3 – Gender-Sensitive Programming reflect this commitment.

title: Charted: How could rising temperatures affect your city?
content: Rising heat could bring more frequent and longer heat waves, higher cooling demand, and greater disease risks for major cities, with impacts worsening significantly between 1.5°C and 3°C of global warming.
snippet: Rising heat could bring more frequent and longer heat waves, higher cooling demand, and greater disease risks for major cities, with impacts worsening significantly between 1.5°C and 3°C of global war

title: Potential recognition of flash flood disasters in China’s southwestern mountainous areas considering source supply conditions
content: This study established a method for the potential recognition of flash flood disasters that is adaptable to different spatial scales considering source supply conditions.
snippet: This study established a method for the potential recognition of flash flood disasters that is adaptable to different spatial scales considering source supply conditions.

title: In Texas, Corpus Christi’s water crisis may be a glimpse into the future
content: Corpus Christi faces a severe water crisis after years of drought, failed desalination plans, and heavy industrial use, forcing the city to consider drastic cuts and even limiting petrochemical operations to protect residents’ water supply.
snippet: Corpus Christi faces a severe water crisis after years of drought, failed desalination plans, and heavy industrial use, forcing the city to consider drastic cuts and even limiting petrochemical operat

title: Quantifying global internal displacement risk at the hazard-vulnerability-conflict nexus
content: This study introduces a novel, diagnostic hazard-specific displacement risk index that integrates high-resolution hazard data with social vulnerability metrics and historical displacement records.
snippet: This study introduces a novel, diagnostic hazard-specific displacement risk index that integrates high-resolution hazard data with social vulnerability metrics and historical displacement records.

title: Cooperation between community organizations and condominium management bodies for tsunami disaster preparedness: Evidence from designated evacuation buildings in Japan
content: This study investigates the mechanisms through which collaborative governance between community organizations and residential management bodies enhances tsunami disaster preparedness in coastal urban areas.
snippet: This study investigates the mechanisms through which collaborative governance between community organizations and residential management bodies enhances tsunami disaster preparedness in coastal urban

title: Turkmenistan landscapes: Opportunities for restoration and livelihoods
content: This analytical study aims to identify strategic land restoration opportunities and inform policy and investment planning for resilient landscape management in Turkmenistan.
snippet: This analytical study aims to identify strategic land restoration opportunities and inform policy and investment planning for resilient landscape management in Turkmenistan.

title: New satellite’s observations close blind spot in tsunami science
content: A new satellite, SWOT, captured detailed tsunami wave patterns after the 2025 Kamchatka quake, revealing near‑trench slip and improving scientists’ ability to model tsunami generation and strengthen coastal hazard preparedness.

title: Emergency planners around the country are about to lose access to a critical hurricane evacuation planning tool
content: FEMA’s hurricane‑planning tool HURREVAC may become temporarily inaccessible due to a stalled contract renewal, raising concerns among emergency managers as hurricane season nears, though FEMA insists service will continue uninterrupted.
snippet: FEMA’s hurricane‑planning tool HURREVAC may become temporarily inaccessible due to a stalled contract renewal, raising concerns among emergency managers as hurricane season nears, though FEMA insists

title: Study on the reach and effectiveness of Early Warning Systems in Timor-Leste
content: This study evaluates the effectiveness of early warning systems in Timor-Leste, based on household surveys and focus group discussions across eight municipalities and key informant interviews with national, municipal and community stakeholders.

title: Lagos state goes live with parametric flood insurance policy, protecting up to 4 million people
content: The Lagos State government has contracted a parametric flood-risk insurance policy in the Nigerian insurance market, covering up to 4 million vulnerable people and securing up to US$7.5 million for flood response and recovery.

title: Adverse weather and health plan
content: The Adverse Weather and Health Plan aims to protect individuals and communities from the health effects of adverse weather and to build community resilience.
snippet: The Adverse Weather and Health Plan aims to protect individuals and communities from the health effects of adverse weather and to build community resilience.

title: Commission launches new strategy to tackle rising wildfire threat
content: The European Commission has introduced a new integrated wildfire strategy to address the growing scale and intensity of fires across Europe, driven largely by climate change.
snippet: The European Commission has introduced a new integrated wildfire strategy to address the growing scale and intensity of fires across Europe, driven largely by climate change.

title: Doerr School of Sustainability, Stanford University
content: The Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability is a school at Stanford University focusing on climate change and sustainability. The school also researches many domains of fossil fuel extraction and development.
snippet: The Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability is a school at Stanford University focusing on climate change and sustainability. The school also researches many domains of fossil fuel extraction and deve

title: UK Wildfire 2026 heads to Leeds
content: The 2026 UK Wildfire Conference is from 10-11 November at Headingley Stadium in Leeds. The website has details on how to participate as a delegate, a speaker, a poster presenter or as a sponsor. The deadline is 24 April 2026 for speaker submissions.

title: Precipitation observing network gaps limit climate change impact assessment
content: The article shows that the global network of precipitation gauges is far too sparse to reliably monitor climate change and water risks.
snippet: The article shows that the global network of precipitation gauges is far too sparse to reliably monitor climate change and water risks.
